name: Japanese N3 Grammar
description: JLPT N3 grammar. Fill in the correct grammar point to complete each sentence.

facts.quizQuestion:
食べ過ぎて、イチゴ{___}食べられない。
(I am so full I could not [even] eat a strawberry.)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — 食べ過ぎて、イチゴさえ食べられない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Being too full to eat even a strawberry is an extreme case — さえ highlights the most minimal thing as an example of impossibility.
facts.sentenceRomaji: tabe sugi te, ichigo ___ tabe rare nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am so full I could not [even] eat a strawberry.
facts.grammarPointLabel: さえ — even; only; just

facts.quizQuestion:
食べ物がなくなれば、人間はネズミ{___}食べる。
(If there is no food, humans would [even] eat a mouse.)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — 食べ物がなくなれば、人間はネズミさえ食べる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Eating a mouse is the extreme thing humans might do when desperate — さえ marks it as the surprising outer limit of what's possible.
facts.sentenceRomaji: tabemono ga nakunare ba, ningen ha nezumi ___ taberu
facts.sentenceTranslation: If there is no food, humans would [even] eat a mouse.
facts.grammarPointLabel: さえ — even; only; just

facts.quizQuestion:
ほとんどの人がその存在{___}知らないようなこと
(thing which most people don't [even] know exists)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — ほとんどの人がその存在さえ知らないようなこと
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Most people don't even know this thing exists — さえ marks existence itself as the minimum level of awareness that's being denied.
facts.sentenceRomaji: hotondo no hito ga sono sonzai ___ shira nai you na koto
facts.sentenceTranslation: thing which most people don't [even] know exists
facts.grammarPointLabel: さえ — even; only; just

facts.quizQuestion:
ソースコードを見つけられ{___}しないのに、開発するなんてもってのほかだ。
(they can't [even] -find- the source code, let alone finish developing it!)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — ソースコードを見つけられさえしないのに、開発するなんてもってのほかだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: They can't even find the source code — さえ emphasizes that finding it, the bare minimum step, is beyond them.

sentenceFurigana.t: なんてもってのほかだ。

facts.sentenceRomaji: soosu koodo wo mitsuke rare ___ shi nai noni, kaihatsu suru nante mottenohoka da
facts.sentenceTranslation: they can't [even] -find- the source code, let alone finish developing it!
facts.grammarPointLabel: さえ — even; only; just

facts.quizQuestion:
定規を使って{___}、まっすぐに線が引けない。
([Even] using a ruler, he cannot draw a straight line.)
facts.explanation: さえ (even; only; just) — 定規を使ってさえ、まっすぐに線が引けない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Even with a ruler — the easiest aid available — straight lines are still impossible. さえ marks using a ruler as the minimal helpful thing.
facts.sentenceRomaji: jougi wo tsuka te ___ , massugu ni sen ga hike nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Even] using a ruler, he cannot draw a straight line.
facts.grammarPointLabel: さえ — even; only; just

facts.quizQuestion:
今日の会議でこの議題{___}議論をしましょう。
(Let's make this matter the [central topic] of the discussion at today's meeting,)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — 今日の会議でこの議題を中心に議論をしましょう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: This agenda item is placed at the center of the discussion — を中心に sets something as the focal point around which other things revolve.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kyou no kaigi de kono gidai ___ giron wo shi masho u
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's make this matter the [central topic] of the discussion at today's meeting,
facts.grammarPointLabel: を中心に — center on, in the middle of

facts.quizQuestion:
駅{___}たくさんの商店が集まっている。
([Centered around] the station there are many stores.)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — 駅を中心にたくさんの商店が集まっている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Stores have gathered around the station as their central hub — を中心に describes the station as the focal point of the surrounding commercial area.
facts.sentenceRomaji: eki ___ takusan no shouten ga atsuma te iru
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Centered around] the station there are many stores.
facts.grammarPointLabel: を中心に — center on, in the middle of

facts.quizQuestion:
中国を海外展開計画の{___}議論しましょう
(Let's make China the [center] of our overseas expansion plan discussion)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — 中国を海外展開計画の中心に議論しましょう
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: China is the central focus of the overseas expansion plan — を中心に places China at the core of the discussion.
facts.sentenceRomaji: chuugoku wo kaigai tenkai keikaku no ___ giron shi masho u
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's make China the [center] of our overseas expansion plan discussion
facts.grammarPointLabel: を中心に — center on, in the middle of

facts.quizQuestion:
この地方{___}として、山地は、本州の東北では三列に、同じく西南では二列に分かれて連なっています。
([Centered around] this region, three separate mountain ranges extend towards north-east of Honshu, and two separate mountain ranges extend to the south-western part of Honshu)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — この地方を中心として、山地は、本州の東北では三列に、同じく西南では二列に分かれて連なっています。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: This region is the geographic center around which the mountain ranges extend — を中心 (followed by として) marks it as the axis of the spatial description.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono chihou ___ toshite, sanchi ha, honshuu no touhoku de ha san retsu ni, onajiku seinan de ha ni retsu ni wakare te tsurana te i masu
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Centered around] this region, three separate mountain ranges extend towards north-east of Honshu, and two separate mountain ranges extend to the south-western part of Honshu
facts.grammarPointLabel: を中心に — center on, in the middle of

facts.quizQuestion:
その家のことはすべてメアリーおばさん{___}動いていた。
(Everything in that house [revolved around] Aunt Mary.)
facts.explanation: を中心に (center on, in the middle of) — その家のことはすべてメアリーおばさんを中心に動いていた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Aunt Mary was the central person around whom the whole household revolved — を中心に captures her role as the pivot of the family's daily life.
facts.sentenceRomaji: sono ie no koto ha subete mearii obasan ___ ugoi te i ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Everything in that house [revolved around] Aunt Mary.
facts.grammarPointLabel: を中心に — center on, in the middle of

facts.quizQuestion:
私は日本に行った{___}日本語を勉強した
([When] I was in Japan, I studied Japanese.)
facts.explanation: 時 (when) — 私は日本に行った時日本語を勉強した
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Studying Japanese happened during a general time period in Japan — 時 is the natural choice for a broad 'when' without pinpointing an exact moment.
facts.sentenceRomaji: watashi ha nippon ni i ta ___ nihongo wo benkyou shi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: [When] I was in Japan, I studied Japanese.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 時 — when

facts.quizQuestion:
いつでも都合のいい{___}にお金を返してください。
(Pay me back [when] it's convenient.)
facts.explanation: 時 (when) — いつでも都合のいい時にお金を返してください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Pay back whenever the timing works for you — 時 refers to a general, flexible time occasion rather than a specific scheduled moment.
facts.sentenceRomaji: itsu demo tsugou no ii ___ ni okane wo kaeshi te kudasai
facts.sentenceTranslation: Pay me back [when] it's convenient.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 時 — when

facts.quizQuestion:
この部屋は物置{___}使っている。
(I'm using this room [as] a storeroom.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — この部屋は物置として使っている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: The room is being used in the role of a storeroom — として marks the capacity or function the room is assigned, not its inherent nature.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono heya ha monooki ___ tsuka te iru
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm using this room [as] a storeroom.
facts.grammarPointLabel: として — as; in the capacity of

facts.quizQuestion:
李さんは自動車の会社でエンジニア{___}働いている。
(Mister Lee is working in a car maker firm [as] an engineer.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — 李さんは自動車の会社でエンジニアとして働いている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Mr. Lee works in the role of an engineer — として shows the professional capacity in which he is employed at the company.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ri san ha jidousha no kaisha de enjinia ___ hatarai te iru
facts.sentenceTranslation: Mister Lee is working in a car maker firm [as] an engineer.
facts.grammarPointLabel: として — as; in the capacity of

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は単に冗談{___}それを言った。
(He said it merely [as] a joke.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — 彼は単に冗談としてそれを言った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: He made the comment in the capacity of a joke, not seriously — として frames the statement's intended role or purpose.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare ha tanni joudan ___ sore wo i ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: He said it merely [as] a joke.
facts.grammarPointLabel: として — as; in the capacity of

facts.quizQuestion:
先生{___}働いています。
(I work [as] a teacher.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — 先生として働いています。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Working as a teacher describes the role the speaker fills — として identifies the professional capacity, not a personal quality.
facts.sentenceRomaji: sensei ___ hatarai te i masu
facts.sentenceTranslation: I work [as] a teacher.
facts.grammarPointLabel: として — as; in the capacity of

facts.quizQuestion:
私は医者{___}あなたに言います。
(I'll tell you this [as] a doctor.)
facts.explanation: として (as; in the capacity of) — 私は医者としてあなたに言います。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Speaking in the professional capacity of a doctor gives weight to the advice — として sets the role or standpoint from which the speaker addresses you.
facts.sentenceRomaji: watashi ha isha ___ anata ni ii masu
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'll tell you this [as] a doctor.
facts.grammarPointLabel: として — as; in the capacity of

facts.quizQuestion:
両親の期待{___}女の子が生まれた。
([Contrary to] the parents expectations, a girl was born.)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — 両親の期待に反して女の子が生まれた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: A girl was born, going against what the parents had hoped for — に反して marks the outcome as directly contrary to an expectation or wish.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ryoushin no kitai ___ onnanoko ga umare ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Contrary to] the parents expectations, a girl was born.
facts.grammarPointLabel: に反して — against, contrary to

facts.quizQuestion:
国民の期待{___}政府は年金制度改正法案の強行採決に踏み切った。
([Contrary to] the peoples expectations, the government bulldozed the pension system revision bill through the Diet.)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — 国民の期待に反して政府は年金制度改正法案の強行採決に踏み切った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The government's action was the opposite of what people expected — に反して shows the action went directly against a stated expectation.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kokumin no kitai ___ seifu ha nenkin seido kaisei houan no kyoukou saiketsu ni fumiki ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Contrary to] the peoples expectations, the government bulldozed the pension system revision bill through the Diet.
facts.grammarPointLabel: に反して — against, contrary to

facts.quizQuestion:
研究社員になる希望{___}大学を止めらなければなりませんでした。
(I had to drop out of university [against] my aspirations of becoming a scientist.)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — 研究社員になる希望に反して大学を止めらなければなりませんでした。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Dropping out went against the speaker's own aspiration of becoming a scientist — に反して shows the outcome opposed a personal goal.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kenkyuu shain ni naru kibou ___ daigaku wo tome ra nakere ba nari mase n deshi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: I had to drop out of university [against] my aspirations of becoming a scientist.
facts.grammarPointLabel: に反して — against, contrary to

facts.quizQuestion:
神の意向{___}人間は自然を破壊している。
([Contrary to] God's will, humans are destroying nature.)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — 神の意向に反して人間は自然を破壊している。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Destroying nature goes against God's will — に反して frames humans' behavior as directly in opposition to a stated will or intention.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kami no ikou ___ ningen ha shizen wo hakai shi te iru
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Contrary to] God's will, humans are destroying nature.
facts.grammarPointLabel: に反して — against, contrary to

facts.quizQuestion:
（僕の期待{___}）実のところ物価は毎週上昇している。
(([Against] my expectations) prices are going up every week.（M)
facts.explanation: に反して (against, contrary to) — （僕の期待に反して）実のところ物価は毎週上昇している。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Prices rising weekly is the opposite of what the speaker was hoping for — に反して marks the outcome as going contrary to a personal expectation.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ( boku no kitai ___ ) mi no tokoro bukka ha maishuu joushou shi te iru
facts.sentenceTranslation: ([Against] my expectations) prices are going up every week.（M
facts.grammarPointLabel: に反して — against, contrary to

facts.quizQuestion:
朝寝坊した。{___}仕事に遅れたんだ。
(I overslept. [Due to that], I was late for work.)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — 朝寝坊した。それで仕事に遅れたんだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Links the oversleeping to its direct consequence — the lateness happened as a natural follow-on result.
facts.sentenceRomaji: asanebou shi ta ___ shigoto ni okure ta n da
facts.sentenceTranslation: I overslept. [Due to that], I was late for work.
facts.grammarPointLabel: それで — and then, after that; Due to that

facts.quizQuestion:
３時のバスで行こうよ。{___}いいかい？
(I say we catch the 3 o'clock bus. [Is that alright] with you?)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — ３時のバスで行こうよ。それでいいかい？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Proposes a plan and then asks if that outcome works for the other person — checking whether the arrangement is acceptable.
facts.sentenceRomaji: san ji no basu de iko u yo ___ ii kai?
facts.sentenceTranslation: I say we catch the 3 o'clock bus. [Is that alright] with you?
facts.grammarPointLabel: それで — and then, after that; Due to that

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}どうなりましたか？
([And then] what happened?)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — それでどうなりましたか？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Prompts for the next step in a sequence — asking what came after the previous part of the story.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ___ dou nari mashi ta ka?
facts.sentenceTranslation: [And then] what happened?
facts.grammarPointLabel: それで — and then, after that; Due to that

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}このカードに記入してください。
([Then] you just have to fill out this card.)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — それでこのカードに記入してください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Moves to the final step in a process — after everything else is handled, this is the remaining action.

sentenceFurigana.g: entry (in a form, register, diary, etc.)

facts.sentenceRomaji: ___ kono kaado ni kinyuu shi te kudasai
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Then] you just have to fill out this card.
facts.grammarPointLabel: それで — and then, after that; Due to that

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}これ以上の義務を負わなくてよくなる。
([That] absolves me from further responsibility.)
facts.explanation: それで (and then, after that; Due to that) — それでこれ以上の義務を負わなくてよくなる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: States that a specific result follows from a condition — completing that action is what makes the further obligation go away.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ___ kore ijou no gimu wo owa naku te yoku naru
facts.sentenceTranslation: [That] absolves me from further responsibility.
facts.grammarPointLabel: それで — and then, after that; Due to that

facts.quizQuestion:
このコンピューター１年前に買ったばかり{___}、もうすっかり時代遅れだわ。
([Even though] I bought this computer one year ago, it's already old fashioned.)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — このコンピューター１年前に買ったばかりなのに、もうすっかり時代遅れだわ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Bought only a year ago, yet already outdated — なのに expresses frustration at the gap between the expected and actual situation.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono konpyuutaa ichi nen mae ni ka ta bakari ___ , mou sukkari jidaiokure da wa
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Even though] I bought this computer one year ago, it's already old fashioned.
facts.grammarPointLabel: なのに — but, although (contrast, surprising

facts.quizQuestion:
この情報は当然最新であるべき{___}、実際はそうでない。
(This information is not as up-to-date [as it should be].)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — この情報は当然最新であるべきなのに、実際はそうでない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The information should be current but isn't — なのに highlights the surprising or unsatisfying contrast between what should be and what is.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono jouhou ha touzen saishin de aru beki ___ , jissai ha sou de nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: This information is not as up-to-date [as it should be].
facts.grammarPointLabel: なのに — but, although (contrast, surprising

facts.quizQuestion:
これは新しい靴{___}もう修理する必要がある。
(These new shoes [already] want mending.)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — これは新しい靴なのにもう修理する必要がある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: New shoes needing repair is surprising and annoying — なのに conveys the speaker's frustration that reality doesn't match expectations.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kore ha atarashii kutsu ___ mou shuuri suru hitsuyou ga aru
facts.sentenceTranslation: These new shoes [already] want mending.
facts.grammarPointLabel: なのに — but, although (contrast, surprising

facts.quizQuestion:
ずっと歩いて来たとは、あなたは足が丈夫{___}ちがいない。
(You must have a strong leg to have walked [all the way].)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — ずっと歩いて来たとは、あなたは足が丈夫なのにちがいない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Having walked all the way implies strong legs — なのに connects the evidence to the surprised conclusion in a logical-contrast chain.
facts.sentenceRomaji: zutto arui te ki ta to ha, anata ha ashi ga joubu ___ chigai nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: You must have a strong leg to have walked [all the way].
facts.grammarPointLabel: なのに — but, although (contrast, surprising

facts.quizQuestion:
ジムは目覚めると、自分が部屋に１人{___}気がついた。
(Jim awoke and found himself [alone] in the room.)
facts.explanation: なのに (but, although (contrast, surprising)) — ジムは目覚めると、自分が部屋に１人なのに気がついた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Jim realized he was alone — なのに here introduces the surprising condition he found himself in upon waking.
facts.sentenceRomaji: jimu ha mezameru to, jibun ga heya ni ichi nin ___ kigatsui ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Jim awoke and found himself [alone] in the room.
facts.grammarPointLabel: なのに — but, although (contrast, surprising

facts.quizQuestion:
私のボスは、短気で怒り{___}。　　　（怒りっぽい is conventional.）
(My boss is short-tempered and [tends to] get angry easily.)
facts.explanation: がち (tend to) — 私のボスは、短気で怒りがち。　　　（怒りっぽい is conventional.）
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes a recurring negative pattern — the boss doesn't just get angry sometimes, it's a reliable habit.
facts.sentenceRomaji: watashi no bosu ha, tanki de ikari ___ . ( ikari ppoi is conventional .)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My boss is short-tempered and [tends to] get angry easily.
facts.grammarPointLabel: がち — tend to

facts.quizQuestion:
担当者の好き嫌いによる誤解も場合によっては起こり{___}。
(In some cases, misunderstanding [tends to] occur due to the likes and dislikes of the person in charge.)
facts.explanation: がち (tend to) — 担当者の好き嫌いによる誤解も場合によっては起こりがち。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Marks a likely recurring problem — misunderstandings due to personal bias are framed as something that happens more often than desired.

sentenceFurigana.g: being in charge (of an area of responsibility)

facts.sentenceRomaji: tantou sha no sukikirai niyoru gokai mo baai niyotte ha okori ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: In some cases, misunderstanding [tends to] occur due to the likes and dislikes of the person in charge.
facts.grammarPointLabel: がち — tend to

facts.quizQuestion:
曇り{___}
(mainly [cloudy])
facts.explanation: がち (tend to / prone to) — 曇りがち means weather that is predominantly cloudy. Attaches to noun or verb-stem to mark a recurring tendency.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes weather that leans heavily toward one condition — it's predominantly cloudy as a recurring state.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kumori ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: mainly [cloudy]
facts.grammarPointLabel: がち — tend to / prone to

facts.quizQuestion:
遠慮{___}
([Tends to be] reserved/timid)
facts.explanation: がち (tend to / prone to) — 遠慮がち labels a habitual personality trait of holding back. Often pairs with negative or undesirable tendencies.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Labels a person's consistent behavioral pattern — holding back is their default, repeated tendency.
facts.sentenceRomaji: enryo ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Tends to be] reserved/timid
facts.grammarPointLabel: がち — tend to / prone to

facts.quizQuestion:
お金持ちは貧乏人を見下し{___}である。
(The rich are [apt to] look down upon the poor.)
facts.explanation: がち (tend to) — お金持ちは貧乏人を見下しがちである。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes a social tendency with a negative tone — the rich looking down on the poor is presented as a habitual pattern.
facts.sentenceRomaji: o kanemochi ha binbounin wo mikudashi ___ de aru
facts.sentenceTranslation: The rich are [apt to] look down upon the poor.
facts.grammarPointLabel: がち — tend to

facts.quizQuestion:
全然見込みがないわけ{___}が、あまり期待はしない方がいいでしょう
(it [does not mean] that there is no hope, but you'd better not expect too much)

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.grammarPointLabel: に対して — toward; to; in contrast to; whereas; in regard to; in; per

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: meron wo tabe ___ , koohii wo nomi mashi ta

facts.quizQuestion:
外から{___}撮ってもいいよ と言われたので，外に出て撮影しました。

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:
速さという点では飛行機は船とは{___}。

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:
日本へ行った{___}のころは、日本語がわからなくて困りました。
(When I was [a newcomer] to Japan, I had trouble understanding Japanese.)
facts.explanation: ばかり (have just done something; have just finished doing something’) — 日本へ行ったばかりのころは、日本語がわからなくて困りました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Just having arrived in Japan, the speaker was a newcomer — ばかり marks the period just after coming, when everything was unfamiliar.
facts.sentenceRomaji: nippon he i ta ___ no koro ha, nihongo ga wakara naku te komari mashi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I was [a newcomer] to Japan, I had trouble understanding Japanese.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばかり — have just done something; have just finished doing something’

facts.quizQuestion:
食べた{___}だから、ちょっと待ってください。
(I [just] ate so please wait a bit.)
facts.explanation: ばかり (have just done something; have just finished doing something’) — 食べたばかりだから、ちょっと待ってください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker just ate a moment ago — ばかり expresses the very recent completion of eating, which is why waiting is needed.
facts.sentenceRomaji: tabe ta ___ dakara, chotto ma te kudasai
facts.sentenceTranslation: I [just] ate so please wait a bit.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばかり — have just done something; have just finished doing something’

facts.quizQuestion:
働き始めた{___}のとき、僕も間違えたよ。
(When I had [just] begun working, I made some mistakes too.)
facts.explanation: ばかり (have just done something; have just finished doing something’) — 働き始めたばかりのとき、僕も間違えたよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Just having started work, mistakes were natural — ばかり marks the period immediately after beginning, when everything is new.
facts.sentenceRomaji: hataraki hajime ta ___ no toki, boku mo machigae ta yo
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I had [just] begun working, I made some mistakes too.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ばかり — have just done something; have just finished doing something’

facts.quizQuestion:
2つのことを一度にしよう{___}はいけません。
(Don't [try to] do two things at a time.)
facts.explanation: として (try to) — 2つのことを一度にしようとしてはいけません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: Captures the moment of attempting something — the speaker is actively trying to do two things at once, which is the bad idea being warned against.
facts.sentenceRomaji: 2 tsu no koto wo ichi do ni shiyo u ___ ha ike mase n
facts.sentenceTranslation: Don't [try to] do two things at a time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: として — try to

facts.quizQuestion:
このような（子供の）態度は、自分たちがある程度独立していることを示そう{___}の結果なのである。
(This is because they are [trying to] prove they are somewhat independent.)
facts.explanation: として (try to) — このような（子供の）態度は、自分たちがある程度独立していることを示そうとしての結果なのである。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Frames the behavior as driven by a goal — the children's attitude comes from their effort to appear independent.

sentenceFurigana.g: long sword (as opposed to the shorter katana)

facts.sentenceRomaji: kono you na( kodomo no) taido ha, jibun tachi ga aruteido dokuritsu shi te iru koto wo shimeso u ___ no kekka na no de aru
facts.sentenceTranslation: This is because they are [trying to] prove they are somewhat independent.
facts.grammarPointLabel: として — try to

facts.quizQuestion:
アイスクリームを食べよう{___}、スプーンがないことに気が付いた。これでは食べようがない。どうしよう。
(When they were [trying to] eat ice cream, they noticed they didn't have a spoon. There's no way to eat. What should we do?)
facts.explanation: として (try to) — アイスクリームを食べようとして、スプーンがないことに気が付いた。これでは食べようがない。どうしよう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes the exact moment before an action — just as they were about to eat the ice cream, the missing spoon became a problem.
facts.sentenceRomaji: aisukuriimu wo tabeyo u ___ , supuun ga nai koto ni kigatsui ta. kore de ha tabe you ga nai. dou shiyo u
facts.sentenceTranslation: When they were [trying to] eat ice cream, they noticed they didn't have a spoon. There's no way to eat. What should we do?
facts.grammarPointLabel: として — try to

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}笑い。
(An [artificial] smile.)
facts.explanation: わざとらしい (fake, pretending) — わざとらしい笑い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The smile looks forced and fake. わざとらしい sits before the noun 笑い to describe it as something performed, not genuine.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ___ warai
facts.sentenceTranslation: An [artificial] smile.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わざとらしい — fake, pretending

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の{___}話し方が嫌いだ。
(I don't like his [affected] manner of speaking.)
facts.explanation: わざとらしい (fake, pretending) — 彼のわざとらしい話し方が嫌いだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: His speaking style comes across as put-on and unnatural — わざとらしい captures that affected, over-the-top quality.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare no ___ hanashikata ga kirai da
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't like his [affected] manner of speaking.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わざとらしい — fake, pretending

facts.quizQuestion:
彼のけがするのは、{___}ですね
(It's like he is [faking] his injury.)
facts.explanation: わざとらしい (fake, pretending) — 彼のけがするのは、わざとらしいですね
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The injury seems staged rather than real. わざとらしい expresses that suspicious, too-convenient quality of his behavior.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare no kega suru no ha, ___ desu ne
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's like he is [faking] his injury.
facts.grammarPointLabel: わざとらしい — fake, pretending

facts.quizQuestion:
明日{___}それを済ませてしまうべきです。
(You ought to have done it [by] tomorrow.)
facts.explanation: までに (by __[day, date, time, etc.]__) — 明日までにそれを済ませてしまうべきです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: There's a deadline of tomorrow for finishing it — までに sets a time limit by which the action must be completed.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ashita ___ sore wo sumase te shimau beki desu
facts.sentenceTranslation: You ought to have done it [by] tomorrow.
facts.grammarPointLabel: までに — by __[day, date, time, etc.]__

facts.quizQuestion:
お昼{___}戻らないといけない。
((I) need to be back [by] noon.)
facts.explanation: までに (by __[day, date, time, etc.]__) — お昼までに戻らないといけない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: Noon is the deadline for returning — までに marks a point in time that serves as the cutoff, not just a general 'when.'
facts.sentenceRomaji: ohiru ___ modora nai to ike nai
facts.sentenceTranslation: (I) need to be back [by] noon.
facts.grammarPointLabel: までに — by __[day, date, time, etc.]__

facts.quizQuestion:
書類を夜{___}ださなければなりません。
(Document must be submitted [by] evening.)
facts.explanation: までに (by __[day, date, time, etc.]__) — 書類を夜までにださなければなりません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
facts.grammarNote: The document submission has a cutoff of evening — までに sets evening as the boundary by which the action must be done.
facts.sentenceRomaji: shorui wo yoru ___ dasa nakere ba nari mase n
facts.sentenceTranslation: Document must be submitted [by] evening.
facts.grammarPointLabel: までに — by __[day, date, time, etc.]__

facts.quizQuestion:
野菜を食べ{___}。
(You [must] eat your vegetables.)
facts.explanation: なければなりません (must ____; really should _____; it won't do if you don't ____.) — 野菜を食べなければなりません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Eating vegetables is framed as a firm requirement, not just advice. なければなりません is the polite form for a strong obligation.
facts.sentenceRomaji: yasai wo tabe ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: You [must] eat your vegetables.
facts.grammarPointLabel: なければなりません — must ____; really should _____; it won't do if you don't ____.

facts.quizQuestion:
お店で卵を買わ{___}。
(I [have to] buy eggs at the store.)
facts.explanation: なければなりません (must ____; really should _____; it won't do if you don't ____.) — お店で卵を買わなければなりません。なければなりません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: と伝えていただけませんか
facts.grammarNote: Buying eggs is something that has to happen — it's a necessary errand. なければなりません states that obligation in polite speech.
facts.sentenceRomaji: o mise de tamago wo kawa ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I [have to] buy eggs at the store.
facts.grammarPointLabel: なければなりません — must ____; really should _____; it won't do if you don't ____.

facts.quizQuestion:
毎日日本語を勉強し{___}。
(I [must] study Japanese everyday.)
facts.explanation: なければなりません (must ____; really should _____; it won't do if you don't ____.) — 毎日日本語を勉強しなければなりません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: と伝えていただけませんか
facts.grammarNote: Daily Japanese study is framed as something the speaker must do. なければなりません expresses this as a firm, polite obligation.
facts.sentenceRomaji: mainichi nihongo wo benkyou shi ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I [must] study Japanese everyday.
facts.grammarPointLabel: なければなりません — must ____; really should _____; it won't do if you don't ____.

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは日本の音楽を聞く{___}上手になります。
([By listening to] Japanese music, you will become better.)
facts.explanation: ことで (by doing this　(meant to highlight a method or means)) — あなたは日本の音楽を聞くことで上手になります。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Shows that the action of listening is the method that produces the improvement — the activity itself is what drives the result.
facts.sentenceRomaji: anata ha nippon no ongaku wo kiku ___ jouzu ni nari masu
facts.sentenceTranslation: [By listening to] Japanese music, you will become better.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことで — by doing this　(meant to highlight a method or means

facts.quizQuestion:
退席する{___}実は正しいことをすることになる。
(You may actually be doing the right thing [by walking out].)
facts.explanation: ことで (by doing this　(meant to highlight a method or means)) — 退席することで実は正しいことをすることになる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Frames walking out as the means by which something right gets accomplished — the action is the mechanism behind the outcome.
facts.sentenceRomaji: taiseki suru ___ jitsuha tadashii koto wo suru koto ni naru
facts.sentenceTranslation: You may actually be doing the right thing [by walking out].
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことで — by doing this　(meant to highlight a method or means

facts.quizQuestion:
まいにちれんしゅうする{___}、にほんごがぺらぺらになりました。
(Everyday [by studying] Japanese language, you will be better.)
facts.explanation: ことで (by doing this　(meant to highlight a method or means)) — まいにちれんしゅうすることで、にほんごがぺらぺらになりました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Identifies daily practice as the direct method behind becoming fluent — the activity is what makes the outcome possible.
facts.sentenceRomaji: mai ni chire n shuusuru ___ , ni hon go ga perapera ni nari mashi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Everyday [by studying] Japanese language, you will be better.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことで — by doing this　(meant to highlight a method or means

facts.quizQuestion:
悪魔め！俺様は聖{___}剣で貴様を倒す！
(Foul demon! I will smite you with this [sacred] sword!)
facts.explanation: なる (Used much like the な of na-adjectives - somewhat archaic.) — 悪魔め！俺様は聖なる剣で貴様を倒す！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: しまう (仕舞う・終う・了う)
facts.grammarNote: 聖なる is a poetic/archaic way to say 'sacred' — なる is used here instead of な for a dramatic, literary feel.
facts.sentenceRomaji: akuma me! ore sama ha kiyoshi ___ ken de kisama wo taosu!
facts.sentenceTranslation: Foul demon! I will smite you with this [sacred] sword!
facts.grammarPointLabel: なる — Used much like the な of na-adjectives - somewhat archaic.

facts.quizQuestion:
スター・ウォーズのエピソード４の副題は、「新た{___}希望」である。
(The subtitle of Episode IV of Star Wars is "a [new] hope".)
facts.explanation: なる (Used much like the な of na-adjectives - somewhat archaic.) — スター・ウォーズのエピソード４の副題は、「新たなる希望」である。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The subtitle 'A New Hope' uses 新たなる — an older, more ceremonial way to say 'new,' fitting the epic tone of the film.
facts.sentenceRomaji: sutaa/uoozu no episoodo yon no fukudai ha,‘ arata ___ kibou’ de aru
facts.sentenceTranslation: The subtitle of Episode IV of Star Wars is "a [new] hope".
facts.grammarPointLabel: なる — Used much like the な of na-adjectives - somewhat archaic.

facts.quizQuestion:
うちは毎年９月末の日曜日{___}と決まっているのです。
(It's our rule to [make it] the last Sunday of September every year.)
facts.explanation: にする (To decide on, to choose) — うちは毎年９月末の日曜日にすると決まっているのです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The family has a fixed rule about which day to use, and にする expresses choosing and settling on that specific option.
facts.sentenceRomaji: uchi ha maitoshi kugatsu matsu no nichiyoubi ___ to kima te iru no desu
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's our rule to [make it] the last Sunday of September every year.
facts.grammarPointLabel: にする — To decide on, to choose

facts.quizQuestion:
そのよう{___}と後で困らない？
(if you [do it] this way, won't you be troubled later on?)
facts.explanation: にする (To decide on, to choose) — そのようにすると後で困らない？
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Doing it 'this way' is the choice being made — にする marks the decision to go with a particular option or approach.
facts.sentenceRomaji: sono you ___ to atode komara nai?
facts.sentenceTranslation: if you [do it] this way, won't you be troubled later on?
facts.grammarPointLabel: にする — To decide on, to choose

facts.quizQuestion:
私 は 食べ{___}
(I [am eating] now.)
facts.explanation: て形+いる (て居る) (Vte (Continuing states, experience, repetative events)/ doing currently) — 私 は 食べている
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Eating is happening right now at the moment of speaking — ている attaches to an action in progress to show it is currently ongoing.
facts.sentenceRomaji: watashi ha tabe ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I [am eating] now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: て形+いる — て居る

facts.quizQuestion:
あなた は 見{___}
(You [are looking] now.)
facts.explanation: て形+いる (て居る) (Vte (Continuing states, experience, repetative events)/ doing currently) — あなた は 見ている
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Looking is happening at this moment — ている marks the action as currently in progress, the simplest ongoing-action usage of this form.
facts.sentenceRomaji: anata ha mi ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: You [are looking] now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: て形+いる — て居る

facts.quizQuestion:
とても寒いけれど、彼女はコート{___}で出かけました。
(Although it is very cold, she went out [without] a coat on.)
facts.explanation: なし (without) — とても寒いけれど、彼女はコートなしで出かけました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: She went out in a coatless state — なし attaches to a noun to mean 'without that thing,' describing the absence of the coat.

sentenceFurigana.g: to go out (e.g. on an excursion or outing)

facts.sentenceRomaji: totemo samui keredo, kanojo ha kooto ___ de dekake mashi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Although it is very cold, she went out [without] a coat on.
facts.grammarPointLabel: なし — without

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女はコート{___}で出かけました
(she went out [without] wearing a coat.)
facts.explanation: なし (without) — 彼女はコートなしで出かけました
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: She left wearing no coat — なし marks the coat as something absent, characterizing the state she was in when she went out.

sentenceFurigana.g: to go out (e.g. on an excursion or outing)

facts.sentenceRomaji: kanojo ha kooto ___ de dekake mashi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: she went out [without] wearing a coat.
facts.grammarPointLabel: なし — without

facts.quizQuestion:
朝ごはんを食べ{___}学校へ来ました。
(I came to school [without] eating breakfast.)
facts.explanation: ずに (without) — 朝ごはんを食べずに学校へ来ました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Coming to school without eating breakfast means the eating was skipped — ずに attaches to a verb in negative form to mean 'without doing' that action.
facts.sentenceRomaji: asa gohan wo tabe ___ gakkou he ki mashi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: I came to school [without] eating breakfast.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ずに — without

facts.quizQuestion:
勉強せ{___}テストを受けた。
(I took the test [without] having studied.)
facts.explanation: ずに (without) — 勉強せずにテストを受けた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Taking the test without having studied means studying was skipped — ずに marks studying as the action that was bypassed before the test.
facts.sentenceRomaji: benkyou se ___ tesuto wo uke ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: I took the test [without] having studied.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ずに — without

facts.quizQuestion:
最近ちょっと太ったから毎日ハンバーガーを食べない{___}しました。
(I gained a little weight recently so I've [decided] not to eat hamburgers every day.)
facts.explanation: ことにする (Decide to do something) — 最近ちょっと太ったから毎日ハンバーガーを食べないことにしました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The weight gain prompted a decision to change eating habits. ことにする is used when you make a choice for yourself about what you will do.
facts.sentenceRomaji: saikin chotto futo ta kara mainichi hanbaagaa wo tabe nai ___ shi mashi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: I gained a little weight recently so I've [decided] not to eat hamburgers every day.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことにする — Decide to do something

facts.quizQuestion:
合格{___}ようにもっと勉強しようと決めました。
(In order to pass the exam I decided to study harder.)
facts.explanation: ことにする (Decide to do something) — 合格するようにもっと勉強をすることにしました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: 合格する is the target state the speaker wants to reach. ように sets up the goal in order to justify studying harder.
facts.sentenceRomaji: goukaku ___ you ni motto benkyou shiyo u to kime mashi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: In order to pass the exam I decided to study harder.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことにする — Decide to do something

facts.quizQuestion:
たくさんあって､{___}食べきれません
(There is so much (food) that I [can't possibly] eat it all.)
facts.explanation: とても〜ない (Can`t possibly) — たくさんあって､とても食べきれません
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: あまり　+　動詞(否定形)
facts.grammarNote: The amount of food makes finishing it simply impossible — とても sets up the impossibility reinforced by the negative verb.
facts.sentenceRomaji: takusan a te ､ ___ tabe kire mase n
facts.sentenceTranslation: There is so much (food) that I [can't possibly] eat it all.
facts.grammarPointLabel: とても〜ない — Can`t possibly

facts.quizQuestion:
東京では{___}家なんか買えません。
(I [can't possibly] buy a house in Tokyo.)
facts.explanation: とても〜ない (Can`t possibly) — 東京ではとても家なんか買えません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Tokyo's prices are so high that buying a house there isn't realistic — the impossibility is total, not just difficult.
facts.sentenceRomaji: toukyou de ha ___ ie nanka kae mase n
facts.sentenceTranslation: I [can't possibly] buy a house in Tokyo.
facts.grammarPointLabel: とても〜ない — Can`t possibly

facts.quizQuestion:
主人が仕事で東京にいく{___}った。
([It has been decided that] my husband will go to Tokyo for professional reasons.)
facts.explanation: ことになる (it has been decided/arranged that) — 主人が仕事で東京にいくことになった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The husband going to Tokyo wasn't his own free choice — it was arranged or decided by circumstances, which is what ことになる conveys.
facts.sentenceRomaji: shujin ga shigoto de toukyou ni iku ___ ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: [It has been decided that] my husband will go to Tokyo for professional reasons.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことになる — it has been decided/arranged that

facts.quizQuestion:
留学する{___}りました。
([It has been arranged that] I will study abroad.)
facts.explanation: ことになる (it has been decided/arranged that) — 留学することになりました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Studying abroad was arranged or settled — the speaker presents it as something that came to be decided, not as a personal vow.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ryuugaku suru ___ ri mashi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: [It has been arranged that] I will study abroad.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ことになる — it has been decided/arranged that

facts.quizQuestion:
昨夜、誰がテレビをつけた{___}
(I [wonder] who turned on the TV last night.)
facts.explanation: かしら。 (I wonder if...　（A type of question mark)) — 昨夜、誰がテレビをつけたかしら。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is wondering who left the TV on — a soft, self-directed mystery. かしら gives it a gentle, feminine wondering tone.
facts.sentenceRomaji: sakuya, dare ga terebi wo tsuke ta ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I [wonder] who turned on the TV last night.
facts.grammarPointLabel: かしら。 — I wonder if...　（A type of question mark

facts.quizQuestion:
いくら{___}
(I [wonder] how much it is.)
facts.explanation: かしら。 (I wonder if...　（A type of question mark)) — いくらかしら。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is wondering about the price to themselves. かしら gives this a soft, feminine 'I wonder' quality rather than a direct question.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ikura ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I [wonder] how much it is.
facts.grammarPointLabel: かしら。 — I wonder if...　（A type of question mark

facts.quizQuestion:
多くのアメリカ人{___}に彼は気さくだ。
([Like] many Americans, he is very friendly.)
facts.explanation: と同様 (the same way as, as well as) — 多くのアメリカ人と同様に彼は気さくだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Likens one thing directly to another — he behaves the same way as many Americans, so the comparison calls for this pattern.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ooku no amerika jin ___ ni kare ha kisaku da
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Like] many Americans, he is very friendly.
facts.grammarPointLabel: と同様 — the same way as, as well as

facts.quizQuestion:
少しずつそうなった{___}
(its a gradual thing, [isn't it]?)
facts.explanation: ということですね (と云う事ですね) (your message is that ...) — 少しずつそうなったということですね
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
distractors: ところに、ところへ、ところを
と伝えていただけませんか
facts.targetLanguageWord: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.reading: ということですね (と云う事ですね)
facts.pronunciation: ということですね (と云う事ですね)

facts.sentenceTranslation: its a gradual thing, [isn't it]?

facts.quizQuestion:

(I want a haircut [like] this one in the picture.)

facts.grammarNote: Points to a specific picture as the reference — you want a haircut that looks like the style in the photo.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kono shashin ___ katto ga hoshii
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want a haircut [like] this one in the picture.
facts.grammarPointLabel: のような — 〜の様な

facts.quizQuestion:
そろそろ終わり{___}？

facts.sentenceTranslation: Is it about to end [?]
facts.grammarPointLabel: かい。 — (A type of question mark

facts.quizQuestion:
電車の中での勉強はとても無理なような{___}します。
(I [feel like] it's almost impossible to study on the train.)
facts.explanation: 気がする (to feel, realize) — 電車の中での勉強はとても無理なような気がします。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Expresses a gut feeling about studying on trains — not a fact, but a personal sense that it would be very difficult.
facts.sentenceRomaji: densha no naka de no benkyou ha totemo muri na you na ___ shi masu
facts.sentenceTranslation: I [feel like] it's almost impossible to study on the train.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 気がする — to feel, realize

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: ryokou de iki mashi ta kedo, soko ni ha sumi taku nai you na ___ shi mashi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: I went travelling there, but [realized] I would not like to live there.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 気がする — to feel, realize

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceTranslation: I [felt that] he was a bad person.

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:
そんな{___}はない

facts.grammarNote: The speaker is asserting that something cannot possibly be true. そんな筈はない is a set phrase rejecting an expected outcome as impossible.

facts.sentenceTranslation: That [cannot be supposed to be so].

facts.quizQuestion:
高い物が必ずしも安いものより良い{___}。

facts.sentenceTranslation: Expensive things are [not always] better than cheap things.

facts.quizQuestion:
いつでも自分の思い通りにできる{___}よ。

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.grammarPointLabel: じゅう／ちゅう — throughout, through, during all

facts.quizQuestion:
第一回目試験を落第したから、受け{___}つもりだよ。dai ikkai me shiken wo rakudai shita kara, uke-naosu tsumori da yo.

facts.sentenceRomaji: dai ichi kai me shiken wo rakudai shi ta kara, uke ___ tsumori da yo. dai ikkai me shiken wo rakudai shita kara , uke - naosu tsumori da yo

facts.grammarPointLabel: そうすると — That means, If that's the case, etc

facts.quizQuestion:
今日の天気が昨日のような天気だったら良かった。{___}
(I [wish] today's weather had been as nice as yesterday's.)
facts.explanation: のに (wish) — 今日の天気が昨日のような天気だったら良かった。のに
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: If only today's weather had been as nice — のに at the end of a sentence expresses a wish or longing for an unrealized situation.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kyou no tenki ga kinou no you na tenki da tara yoka ta ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: I [wish] today's weather had been as nice as yesterday's.
facts.grammarPointLabel: のに — wish

facts.quizQuestion:
雨さえやめばいい{___}なあ。
([If only] it would stop raining!)
facts.explanation: のに (wish) — 雨さえやめばいいのになあ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: If only the rain would stop — のに expresses a heartfelt wish for something to be different from the current situation.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ame sae yame ba ii ___ naa

facts.grammarPointLabel: のに — wish

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたは{___}とても若い。
(you are [still] very young.)

facts.grammarNote: Emphasizes ongoing youth — you haven't aged out of anything yet, you're still very young right now.

facts.grammarPointLabel: 未だ — yet / as yet / so far/ not yet

facts.quizQuestion:
宿題を{___}終わっていない。

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Describes an unfinished task — the homework was supposed to be done, but it still isn't, even now.
facts.sentenceRomaji: shukudai wo ___ owa te i nai

facts.grammarPointLabel: 未だ — yet / as yet / so far/ not yet

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.grammarNote: The quick thinking happened automatically — there was no time to plan; the speaker's wits kicked in on their own.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ___ no kiten de, sainan wo nogareru koto ga deki ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: Due to my [instinctive] quick wits, I was able to escape the calamity.
facts.grammarPointLabel: とっさに — To reflexively react; an unconscious movement.

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の反応は{___}出たものだ。
(His response is [automatic].)
facts.explanation: とっさに (To reflexively react; an unconscious movement.) — 彼の反応はとっさに出たものだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: His response was purely instinctual — it didn't come from deliberate thought, it just came out automatically.
facts.sentenceRomaji: kare no hannou ha ___ de ta mono da
facts.sentenceTranslation: His response is [automatic].
facts.grammarPointLabel: とっさに — To reflexively react; an unconscious movement.

facts.quizQuestion:
何よりも、満員電車に乗ら{___}のはうれしい。
(Above anything, employees are happy for [not having to] ride on overcrowded trains.)
facts.explanation: なくてもすむ (Don't have to 〜　(and are happy because of it)) — 何よりも、満員電車に乗らなくてもすむのはうれしい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Employees are happy because they don't have to ride crowded trains — なくてもすむ means they can get by, or manage fine, without having to do it.

facts.sentenceRomaji: nani yori mo, man'in densha ni nora ___ no ha ureshii
facts.sentenceTranslation: Above anything, employees are happy for [not having to] ride on overcrowded trains.
facts.grammarPointLabel: なくてもすむ — Don't have to 〜　(and are happy because of it

facts.quizQuestion:
今度の試験は易しいから、あまり勉強し{___}。
(The upcoming test should be easy, so I think I can get by [without studying much].)

facts.grammarNote: The easy test means you can manage without studying much — なくてもすむ shows you can get through the situation without needing to do that action.

facts.quizQuestion:
うちの息子{___}、全然勉強しないで、ゲームばっかりしてるのよ。
([Speaking of] my son, he is always playing games without studying.)
facts.explanation: ときたら (when it comes to~ (neg. sense)) — うちの息子ときたら、全然勉強しないで、ゲームばっかりしてるのよ。

facts.grammarNote: The speaker is raising my son as a (negative) topic — ときたら introduces a topic with a critical or exasperated tone, signaling a complaint is coming.
facts.sentenceRomaji: uchi no musuko ___ , zenzen benkyou shi nai de, geemu bakkari shi teru no yo
facts.sentenceTranslation: [Speaking of] my son, he is always playing games without studying.

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.sentenceRomaji: watashi ___ , ryouri ha mattaku dame na no yo

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.grammarNote: What's good for the mother's body flows through and benefits the baby too — ひいては shows how one effect extends to a downstream result.

facts.sentenceRomaji: sore ha botai ni yoku, ___ onaka no akachan ni mo yoi
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is good for the mother's body, [which in turn] is good for her coming baby.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}国際化に繋がるのだ ろうか?
(Will this [in turn] lead to internationalization?)

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The first effect might lead further to internationalization — ひいては connects the immediate outcome to its broader, downstream consequence.
facts.sentenceRomaji: ___ kokusai ka ni tsunagaru no da rou ka ?

facts.quizQuestion:

facts.quizQuestion:
２人っきりでデート・・{___}、ＯＦＦ会だと思ったけど、そこまで甘くもないよな。

facts.quizQuestion:
Q. ピクニックは どこが　いいですか？　A. どこ{___} いいです。
(Q. Where is the best spot for a picnic? A. [Anywhere] will do.)
facts.explanation: Q~でも (Question word + demo = Question word + ever. (Whenever, wherever, whatever, whoever)) — Q. ピクニックは どこが　いいですか？　A. どこでも いいです。

facts.grammarNote: どこでも means any place will work — the question asked where, and the answer is 'wherever, it doesn't matter.'
facts.sentenceRomaji: Q . pikunikku ha doko ga ii desu ka? A . doko ___ ii desu
facts.sentenceTranslation: Q. Where is the best spot for a picnic? A. [Anywhere] will do.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Q~でも — Question word + demo = Question word + ever. (Whenever, wherever, whatever, whoever

facts.quizQuestion:
能力{___}
([lack of] ability.)

facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: Lack of ability is expressed by nominalizing 'no ability' into a noun phrase — のなさ turns an adjective or verb into a noun meaning 'the absence of X.'
facts.sentenceRomaji: nouryoku ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: [lack of] ability.
facts.grammarPointLabel: のなさ — lack of something

facts.quizQuestion:
こんな大きなケーキをよくもひとりで食べられる{___}
([How] could he eat a cake that big all by himself?)
facts.explanation: よくも〜ものだ (How the hell is it possible, that ...?) — こんな大きなケーキをよくもひとりで食べられるものだ
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.grammarNote: The speaker is amazed — eating a whole cake that big, alone, seems almost impossible. よくも～ものだ expresses that disbelief and mild outrage.
facts.sentenceRomaji: konna ookina keeki wo yokumo hitori de tabe rareru ___
facts.sentenceTranslation: [How] could he eat a cake that big all by himself?
facts.grammarPointLabel: よくも〜ものだ — How the hell is it possible, that ...?

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}の果てに彼は刑務所行きになった。
([In the end] he landed up in jail.)
facts.explanation: 挙げ句 (in the end, after all) — 挙げ句の果てに彼は刑務所行きになった。

facts.quizQuestion:

([After] hard thinking, I carried out the plan.)

sentenceFurigana.g: to transfer (to a different place, group, etc.)

facts.sentenceRomaji: sanzan kangae ta ___ sono keikaku wo jikkou ni utsushi ta
facts.sentenceTranslation: [After] hard thinking, I carried out the plan.
facts.grammarPointLabel: 挙げ句 — in the end, after all

synonymGroups.reason: から and ので are often interchangeable for 'because', but ので is softer and more objective; から is more direct and subjective
らしい, よう, みたい, そう(hearsay), の様 all express 'seems/apparently' but differ in formality, information source, and directness of inference
なければならない, せねばならない, なければなりません are near-synonyms for obligation, differing mainly in formality and register
ことにする (1629 and 1769 are the same pattern, different IDs — volitional decision) vs ことになる (external/natural result that has been decided)
しか (requires negative verb), さえ (even/only — extremity marker), だけ (just/only — neutral), ばかり (nothing but/just did), こそ (emphasis on this one thing)
